subject: DevOps
title: Blue-Green and Canary Deployments
description: A micro-lesson that makes Blue-Green and Canary Deployments usable.

hook.text: A bad deploy took down the site for an hour. Blue-green would have made rollback instant.

buildup.text: Blue-green runs two environments. Blue has the current version. Green gets the new one. Swap traffic.

discovery.text: If green breaks, switch back to blue. Zero-downtime rollback since blue is still running.

twist.text: Canary is more gradual. Route 5% to the new version, watch metrics, then ramp to 100%.

climax.text: Cloud load balancers make both patterns simple. Weighted routing shifts traffic with one config.

punchline.text: Blue-green for instant rollback. Canary for gradual safety.

quiz.question: How does a canary deployment reduce risk?
options: By routing only a small percentage of traffic to the new version first
By deploying only to servers in one geographic region
By running automated tests instead of deploying
